title: Mexican Nduja Empanadas
description: A spicy twist on traditional empanadas, filled with Nduja sausage and wrapped in crispy pastry.
instructions: Roll out pastry dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
In a pan over medium heat, cook the Nduja sausage until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in chopped cilantro and cook for 1 minute.
Spoon the Nduja mixture onto one half of each dough circle, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the dough over the filling and press the edges together with a fork to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with lime juice and sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
